truth.papers.A23.title: Evolution of microstructure, texture and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–11Al–1.2C low-density steel during cold rolling
truth.papers.A23.paper_scope: Systematically investigate the evolution of microstructure, texture and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–11Al–1.2C low-density steel during cold rolling up to 90% reduction.

prediction.papers.A23.title: Evolution of microstructure, texture and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–11Al–1.2C low-density steel during cold rolling
prediction.papers.A23.paper_scope: Experimental study of microstructure, crystallographic texture and mechanical property evolution in Fe-30Mn-11Al-1.2C austenitic low-density steel during cold rolling up to 90% thickness reduction.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn11al1_2c.alloys_notes: SFE estimated as ~108 mJ/m2. Austenitic matrix contains nano-sized κ'-carbides formed via spinodal decomposition during quenching.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_st.descrip: Solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h (as-quenched state)
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_cr10.descr: Solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h followed by cold rolling to 10% reduction
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_cr20.descr: Solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h followed by cold rolling to 20% reduction
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_cr30.descr: Solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h followed by cold rolling to 30% reduction

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_cr90.descr: Solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h followed by cold rolling to 90% reduction

prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_st.descrip: Vacuum induction melting; homogenization at 1250°C for 12 h; hot forging to 60 mm diameter bars with finish forging temperature 900°C; solution treatment at 1100°C for 1 h; water quenched (as-quenched state).
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn11al1_2c_st.process: Base material preparation prior to cold rolling.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_st.processing_st: Forged into bars 60 mm in diameter; finish forging temperature 900°C.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr10.processing_: Base material prepared by shared steps in proc_fe30mn11al12c_01 prior to cold rolling.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_cr20.processing_: Base material prepared by shared steps in proc_fe30mn11al12c_01 prior to cold rolling.

truth.structures.sample_st.overall_structure: Equiaxed austenite grains with annealing twins and uniformly distributed nano-sized κ'-carbides. Grain size ~70 μm (including twins). Texture nearly random.
truth.structures.sample_cr10.overall_structure: Taylor lattice structure consisting of multiple slip bands; planar dislocation glide dominates. Slip band spacing varies among grains, indicating inhomogeneous deformation.
truth.structures.sample_cr10.microstructure_li: Taylor lattice (slip bands, dislocation pile-ups at intersections)
truth.structures.sample_cr20.overall_structure: Copper-type rolling texture starting to develop; microstructure consists of refined Taylor lattice (slip bands).
truth.structures.sample_cr30.overall_structure: Taylor lattice with average slip band spacing 231 nm; organized planar slip bands.
truth.structures.sample_cr30.microstructure_li: Taylor lattice (average slip band spacing 231 nm)
truth.structures.sample_cr40.overall_structure: Refined slip bands; copper-type texture near maximum intensity.
truth.structures.sample_cr50.overall_structure: Appearance of microbands (average width 166 nm) and nano-sized deformation twins (average thickness 5.1 nm). Slip bands refined to ~64 nm spacing; planar glide still dominant but cross slip may occur at intersections.
truth.structures.sample_cr50.microstructure_co: deformed austenite with microbands and twins
truth.structures.sample_cr50.microstructure_li: high (geometrically necessary dislocations at microband boundaries)
truth.structures.sample_cr70.overall_structure: Presence of twins (average thickness increased to 19.2 nm), shear bands consisting of ultrafine grains, and dislocation tangles (average size 0.49 μm). Grains elongated along RD.
truth.structures.sample_cr70.microstructure_co: deformed austenite with shear bands and twins
truth.structures.sample_cr70.microstructure_li: elongated grains with shear bands and twins
truth.structures.sample_cr90.overall_structure: Dislocation cell structure and shear bands; grains heavily elongated; ultrahigh strength achieved. Cross slip activation leads to wavy glide.
truth.structures.sample_cr90.microstructure_li: Dislocation tangles/cells (average size 0.49 μm, as seen in TEM)

prediction.structures.sample_st.overall_structure: Austenitic matrix containing nano-sized κ'-carbides uniformly distributed and a large amount of annealing twins; grain size ~70 μm including twins; almost random texture.
prediction.structures.sample_cr10.overall_structure: Taylor-lattice-type low energy dislocation structure (LEDS) formed by planar dislocation glide; slip bands with dislocation pile-ups at intersections; inhomogeneous deformation.
prediction.structures.sample_cr30.overall_structure: Taylor lattice with increased slip bands; average slip band spacing ~231 nm; planar dislocation glide dominates.
prediction.structures.sample_cr30.microstructure_li: deformed matrix with refined slip bands
prediction.structures.sample_cr50.overall_structure: Microbands with average width ~166 nm; nano-deformation twins with average thickness ~5.1 nm and high internal dislocation density; single slip bands hardly distinguishable.
prediction.structures.sample_cr50.microstructure_li: deformed matrix with microbands and nano-twins
prediction.structures.sample_cr70.overall_structure: Twins size increased to ~19.2 nm; extensive shear bands comprising ultrafine grains confirmed by diffraction ring pattern; larger localized strain concentration.
prediction.structures.sample_cr70.microstructure_li: matrix with twins and shear bands
prediction.structures.sample_cr90.overall_structure: Dislocation tangles structure with average size ~0.49 μm; shear bands increased and eroded almost all matrix; grains visibly elongated along rolling direction.
prediction.structures.sample_cr90.microstructure_li: elongated grains with dislocation cells/tangles and shear bands

truth.interfaces.sample_cr50.defect_interactio: high dislocation density within twins; twin boundaries impede dislocation glide
truth.interfaces.sample_cr50.interface_notes[0: local stress concentration at twin intersections may promote further twinning
truth.interfaces.sample_cr50.phase_evolution: Deformation twins nucleate in austenite at 50% reduction and grow with further rolling.
truth.interfaces.sample_cr70.phase_evolution: twins coarsen to ~19.2 nm; shear bands develop at twin/matrix lamellae constraints.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr50.key: Start of copper-type to brass-type transition; Goss and Brass components increase; CuT detected.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr70.key: Goss component increases rapidly; Cu component decreases; weak γ-fiber appears.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_cr90.key: Brass-type texture dominant; α-fiber fully developed; Goss orientation strong; weak γ-fiber retained.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_st.key_f: Room temperature test along RD with strain rate 10^-3 s^-1; YS ~570 MPa, UTS ~830 MPa, uniform elongation >75%.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr10.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~800 MPa, UTS ~1050 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr20.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~1000 MPa, UTS ~1250 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr30.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~1200 MPa, UTS ~1450 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr40.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~1300 MPa, UTS ~1550 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr50.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~1500 MPa, UTS ~1750 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr70.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~1800 MPa, UTS ~2050 MPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_cr90.key: Room temperature test along RD; YS ~2000 MPa, UTS ~2100 MPa; material lost work hardening capability.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated critical twinning stress 1.48 GPa exceeds resolved shear stress 0.75 GPa, yet twinning observed; attributed to SRO and local stress concentration.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Critical resolved shear stress for deformation twinning (Eq. 1)
γSF = 108 mJ/m^2, bp = 0.146 nm → τc ≈ 1.48 GPa
Used to evaluate twinning possibility; nominal critical stress higher than applied, but twinning occurs due to SRO effects and local stress.
K: geometrical factor, M: Taylor factar, G: shear modulus, b: Burgers vector, D: average slip band distance
Explains long-range interaction strengthening from coplanar slip bands; later microbands, twins, shear bands and dislocation tangles provide Hall-Petch strengthening.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Critical shear stress for twinning calculated as ~1.48 GPa was compared with maximum resolved shear stress ~0.75 GPa; actual twinning occurrence was explained by short range order (SRO) and local stress concentration.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Critical shear stress for deformation twinning
Estimate τc from SFE and partial Burgers vector
τc = 2γSF / bp; calculated τc ~1.48 GPa, which is higher than the resolved shear stress ~0.75 GPa.
Strengthening from coplanar slip band interaction
σTL = K·M·G·b/D; strengthening attributed to long range interaction between coplanar slip bands rather than dynamic Hall-Petch effects.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Stacking fault energy (SFE) of this steel estimated as ~108 mJ/m².
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: In the as-quenched state, uniform elongation exceeds 75%, demonstrating excellent work hardening capability.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: True stress at necking point is similar for low to medium reduction (<50%) but sharply enhanced at higher reductions.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Texture transition from copper-type to brass-type occurs at 50% rolling reduction, characterized by decrease of Cu and S components and increase of Brass and Goss.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The presence of short range order (SRO) in austenitic matrix and inhomogeneous local stress concentration may reduce the critical twinning stress enabling twin formation despite high SFE.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Dislocation cross slip becomes prominent at 90% reduction, leading to dislocation cell structure and wavy glide.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: As-quenched yield strength from text (~570 MPa) differs from value in engineering stress-strain table (600 MPa at 0% strain).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The initial microstructure contains κ'-carbides formed via spinodal decomposition during quenching; these are not mentioned after cold rolling, possibly dissolved or still present but not analyzed.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Average slip band spacing at 30% and 50% reduction: 231 nm and 64 nm, respectively.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Strengthening mechanisms: Taylor lattice by long-range interaction; later substructures (microbands, twins, shear bands, dislocation tangles) by Hall-Petch type grain refinement.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: With cold rolling, the true stress at the necking point was similar at low to medium rolling reductions (<50%), while it sharply increased during further rolling.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The rolling texture transitioned from copper-type to brass-type after 50% cold rolling reduction, attributed to the combination of dislocation planar glide and deformation twinning.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Deformation twinning occurred during cold rolling despite a high stacking fault energy (~108 mJ/m2), explained by short range order (SRO) in the austenitic matrix and local stress concentration lowering the actual critical shear stress.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The α-fiber texture intensity monotonously increased up to 90% rolling reduction, while the γ-fiber remained weak with slight strengthening at 90% reduction near the {111}〈110〉 E component.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Along the β-fiber, the Cu orientation increased first and then decreased, the S orientation behaved similarly but decreased later, and the Goss orientation monotonously increased, indicating a continuous shift from Brass towards Goss with rolling reduction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The deformation substructure evolution sequence with increasing rolling reduction was Taylor lattice → microbands → nano-twins → shear bands → dislocation cells, indicating a change from planar to wavy dislocation glide.
